transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hey, Tyler.
Good morning.
How are you?
I'm good.
I'm good.
Where are you calling in from?
I'm calling from Toronto.
What about you?
Okay, nice.
That's high up on my list of places to travel.
I still have never been to Canada.
I'm in Houston.
Okay, great.
I've never been to USA besides Las Vegas, so I really have to travel there as well.
Yeah, mean, you're so close to New York City in Toronto, so I'm sure that's a place you want to go to at some point.
that's very true.
Yeah.
But it's still like a six-hour drive, I think, or so.
But yeah, I have to get there definitely.
It's been only one year I live there, so.
Okay, nice.
Nice.
Where are you from originally, if not Toronto?
From France.
Okay, cool.
Cool.
Well, great to be in touch on behalf of the intro from Alex.
I with you more about GrowthX today and, you know, just tell you about our partnership model, the way that we work.
Would love like a quick intro from you.
It looks like you guys haven't created too much like organic content besides like the main landing pages people land on with finding phone numbers, finding emails.
Yeah, just tell me a little bit more about the journey and what you're thinking about from a content perspective.
Yeah, sure.
So we are a prospeo, right?
We help company finding contact information.
We are like precisely focused on enriching email and mobiles.

And it's been one year, two years now we are building the version two of prospeo, which is like a whole new product that allows you to find the leads at first instead of just having to just be being able to enrich them down the road.
And this product comes with like a lot of new content.
mean, the whole website will be revamped.
The thing is we had a lot.

of blogs that were generating a lot of traffic, but we had some issue with LinkedIn, where we used some trademarks, we used the LinkedIn wars very often, and we had some legal trouble with LinkedIn, so we had to simply remove the blog.

Also, we are working on a programmatic SEO strategy with a directory of people and company, which means that the blog would have gone down anyway, because when you work on programmatic at that scale, you cannot have a blog.
It's not sustainable.
The blog could even impact negatively the programmatic SEO, because those two things are very, very different from Google's regard.
In our opinion, that's what we observed from many competitors.
So yeah, so we removed the blog.
So yeah, right now we don't have any blog.
We don't plan to make one as well.
We are entirely focused on programmatic SEO that's coming very soon.
But where we need your help, guys, is mostly on the AI part, you
But we would like to rank higher on AI.
That's about it.
Okay, sounds good.
Do you need help executing on the programmatic use case?
Or is that something that your team is going to do in-house?
Yeah, it's free in-house.
We are ready for that.
Got it.
And I guess where did you imagine that we would execute on increasing your AI search visibility?
Because we would be creating content, I would assume, to do that, correct?
Okay, so that's where the discussion is for us.
We don't exactly know how AI ranking works.
That's not in my scope of work.
mean, that's not in my skill set, I would say.
So basically, I know nothing about it.
If we could do it without having to create content, would be great for us.
Because I've been approached by some agencies that claim that they can do it from user-generated content already.
For example, for chattypt that ingest their data from...
Or from other sources where each AI get their information from different sources and stuff like that.
Does that make sense?
Or is it like completely a dream?
No, that makes sense.
I think I can give you like a quick overview of the way we work.
And that'll give you a better idea of where we can fit in.
So let me just share my screen here.
Perfect.
So GrowthX, typically what we're doing is end to end, we're helping you guys out as like an extension of your content team.
So SEO and GEO strategy, creating content, staging and publishing in your CMS, all of the LLM visibility and analytics.
That's how we're working with each of our clients.
And so we create content, right, on our client's behalf.
Typically on their website, we are typically starting with editorial.
So a lot of times it is on the blog, and then we're looking at like programmatic work streams, similar to what you have in mind.
But from our perspective, you know, the approach isn't that different when you think about SEO and GEO.
GEO, it's more about are you answering all the questions that your audience might have, and are you creating content at a high enough scale with enough quality to start showing up, right?
So there's on-domain and there's off-domain, Reddit, you know, places like that.
I would say that, you know, for us, like Monograph is one of our customers here, and the type of content that we're creating, like it's benefiting both sides of search.
And the reason that, you we're seeing that, and obviously there's a reason to invest in GEO, the first sales call is happening in ChatGPT or something similar.
AI search visits, they're more likely to convert, they come in a lot more educated.

And so, you know, what we're doing here is, like, we're creating these very complex workflows with experts in the loop so that we can basically create content that is set up to give you the most likelihood to show up in AI search.
That's, like, a little bit around, like, what we do and where we fit in.
Any questions that you have for me?
Just eye level?
No, that's very clear for now.
Do you have more to say about this thing?
Yeah, definitely.
You know, I think part of our attraction is Marcel.
He's always believed brands should be publishers and has built content engines at every step in his journey.
At IBM, you know, they created a microsite called securityintelligence.com that was their highest ROI marketing channel for several years.
And then he was the marketing leader at HashiCorp from $6 to $100 million, at Scale.ai as they got acquired by Meta.
And then at Deepgram, he replicated that.
That process, right, where you build a big team and, you know, you think about, right, first, what's the assignment I want to create content from?
How do I build a really robust brief for that assignment?
How do I do deep research and then fact check the content?
He basically replicated all those steps in the process at DeepGram, had some really great results, started hosting these workshops, and that's what led to the start of GrowthX.

So it's really, like, having this background, right, where, like, Marcel and the team that we've hired on our delivery org, they've always, right, been creating content at scale, have backgrounds in SEO.
And so it is, like, that blend of AI workflows, experts in the loop, and all of the work that we do is powered by our own platform.
So we've got, like, our own proprietary platform, we're working over the next couple of months to make that client-facing, and then...

everybody from our team, whether it's like our chief content officer who was editor-in-chief at TechCrunch for several years or folks on our delivery org like Jacob who built out the G2.com site, it's really like combining those two pieces so that your team doesn't have to worry about like creating the content.
You could focus your efforts on maybe more strategic pillar pieces or even channels outside of content.
So it sounds like you're looking for more of like a strategic partner as opposed to like an end-to-end partner.
Is that fair to say?
Not necessarily, to be honest.
Like we are very specific about what we need and we know it's not content because, I mean, you know, it's a strategy we have been working on for like a year now.
We know that some companies run exclusively on programmatic SEO with directories of people like RocketWitch, Lucha, for example.
RocketWitch generate literally a hundred million of revenue per year with...

Only programmatic SEO, like that's their one source of revenue, have literally zero marketing otherwise, and their product is , but they just rely on the fact that you end up on their product if you type any name on Google.

So we know that content won't be the strategy that will push it, because the programmatic SEO will put all our blogs, all our content in the abyss, we know that in the limbs, we know that because it happened with many competitors that try to do programmatic SEO without success.
But we build a team that we know will make programmatic SEO working, because we have like some experts that have been working at this company and stuff.
So basically, we don't want to create any content.
I know it's kind of specific, and it's tricky for you to work with this situation, but we don't want to create any content.

Or we can do it if you feel it's like really relevant, but like you have to understand that this content won't generate any traffic, that's 100% sure, because the programmatic SEO will kill the content.
In any possible way.
Google will think we are spammers, but we will go after keywords that have solo searches, such as name and last name, that will rank on those anyway, even though we have a very, very bad SEO.
Google sees us very, very badly SEO-wise, if that makes sense.
So, yeah, we're more focused on the, you know, how to work on AI without posting content.

But if you think it's necessary, we can, but we would feel kind of like we are wasting, you know, burning money because we would pay you guys to work on the content strategy, whereas nobody would ever land on those pages.
See what I mean?
I see what you mean.
I guess it's just how do you anticipate showing up on AI search without content, right?
I feel like that's such a key piece in the equation.
Yes, you can influence Reddit posts and third-party channels, but LLMs are going to be looking across your website primarily, right, when it comes to the content that'll show up.
So for the content and all, of course, we'll optimize the website as much as possible.
We can also build pages that we don't build them for ranking, but instead for LLM to gather information about us and stuff like that.
But for example, right now, it's been like, I think about eight months we removed our blog and we do get a lot of customers from AI.
I don't know why we never did anything about that.
So I'm pretty sure there are still some area of improvements.
If we can get customers without having any blogs from eight months, I believe we can maybe find a way to generate traffic from LLM without having blogs.
But, you know, you're the expert.
Like, you tell me about it.
To be honest, I don't know much about it.
Yeah, no worries.
You know, I think it may just not be the best fit because for every client that we bring on, we set up our engagements where we've got this eight-week strategy sprint.
it may just be everybody else.
Thank
And the strategy team, you know, they're more of the experts on GEO, right?
Like I'm on the sales side and improving my knowledge as fast as I can.
But the folks that work on the strategy sprint team, like they're the SEO and GEO experts.
And a lot of the deliverables are around the opportunity for AI search.

When you think about phase two of our engagements and every new client that we sign, we're, you know, in this privileged position where like, we can kind of choose who we work with because we don't say yes to everybody and then do a bad job at scale, right?
Every client we work with, they want us to generate content at scale.
And we target like five net new pieces per week.
So I think, you know, Marcel, like, I've heard him talk about how important the editorial foundation.
And you're absolutely right that, like, if you go programmatic at scale, it will affect the editorial content that you create.
But I think the way we would be.
partner for you, the way it would make sense is if you needed us to actually execute on that programmatic use case, but it's not really what you need, you know?
No, because it's very specific.
It's 100 million of pages.
There are like 10 people in the world that know exactly how to do this kind of job.
So it's not our programmatic SEO.
So for this reason, I don't think you guys would be qualified to help us in this exact use case.
And I also understand that you guys like link together the content and the AI work.
So indeed, it sounds like it wouldn't be a great fit.
But I will say, I think early next year, we're launching a product.
It's all about AI visibility.
So I think whenever that comes out, definitely want to reach out to you.
But yeah, like all the examples that I would show you here that we've created for clients are like these business travel guides, these guides for lovable, these guides for augment code.
I'll share some of these examples, but you can see.
See like the level that we get to with some of our content, you know, like we're putting code snippets into the content with augmented code.
There's crosslinking, there's tables, infographics.
We've also done programmatic plays.
So we are building out Lovable's templates library and making sure that each page is, you know, SDO, GEO optimized.
We've also built out all the vendor directory pages for Ramp where we've got their internal spend data populated into each of these reviews and graphs.
That's great.
And then I can dig into like the workflows themselves as well.
But yeah, you have a very clear idea of what you want to execute on.
And I think we can help you with GEO strategy, but if it wasn't connected to some sort of content deliverable, it probably wouldn't make sense.
Yeah, 100%.
What you do is fantastic, by the way.
We would definitely go with you guys if like the content we did align.

Maybe if you guys provide more, but yeah, I'm sorry, I don't think it's a great fit right now, but thank you very much for your time and, you know, taking time to understand our needs and giving us some suggestions and stuff.
appreciate that a lot.
And yeah, sorry, it didn't work out.
Yeah, no problem.
Let's stay in touch.
And you guys have a really awesome use case in mind, so wish you all the best in executing it.
Thank you very much.
Have a good one.
Nice to meet you, Hugo.
Nice to meet you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[To explore a potential partnership for Prospeo's AI search visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=173.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Prospeo's Strategy:** A hyper-focused programmatic SEO play (100M+ pages) to capture high-intent, long-tail search traffic (e.g., "first name last name").](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=518.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Model:** An end-to-end content agency where AI search visibility (GEO) is a direct outcome of creating high-quality editorial content at scale.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=253.0)
  - [**Strategic Mismatch:** Prospeo's strategy explicitly avoids editorial content, as it would be outranked by the programmatic pages and negatively impact Google's perception of the site.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=518.0)
  - [**Outcome:** No partnership. GrowthX's model is incompatible with Prospeo's content-free strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=708.0)

## Topics

### Prospeo's Strategy: Programmatic SEO

  - [Prospeo is launching v2 of its product, shifting from contact enrichment to lead generation.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=98.0)
  - [The core marketing strategy is programmatic SEO:](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=134.0)
      - [**Goal:** Build a directory of 100M+ people/companies.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=134.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Capture high-intent, long-tail search traffic (e.g., "first name last name").](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=598.0)
      - [**Model:** Emulates companies like RocketReach, which generates \~$100M/year from this strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=518.0)
  - [**Content Rationale:** Prospeo removed its blog after legal issues with LinkedIn and will not create new editorial content.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=118.0)
      - [**Why:** Editorial content would be outranked by the programmatic pages and negatively impact Google's perception of the site.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
      - [**Execution:** The programmatic SEO build is handled in-house by a specialized team.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=186.0)

### GrowthX's Model: Content-Driven GEO

  - [GrowthX is an end-to-end content agency that integrates AI search visibility (GEO) with traditional SEO.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=253.0)
  - [**Process:** An 8-week strategy sprint followed by ongoing content creation (\~5 pieces/week).](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=708.0)
  - [**Core Principle:** GEO is a direct outcome of creating high-quality editorial content at scale.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=343.0)
  - [**Team:** Founded by Marcel (ex-HashiCorp, Scale.ai, Deepgram) and includes experts like a former TechCrunch editor-in-chief.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=390.0)
  - [**Platform:** Uses a proprietary platform to manage workflows, with a client-facing version launching early next year.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=463.0)

### The Strategic Mismatch

  - [Prospeo's need was for AI search visibility *without* creating editorial content.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=198.0)
  - [GrowthX's model is built on content creation, making it an incompatible fit.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=253.0)
  - [**Prospeo's View:** Creating content would be a waste of money, as it would be outranked and negatively impact the programmatic strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=577.0)
  - [**GrowthX's View:** Content is a necessary input for AI models to surface information about a brand.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:** Share GrowthX's client examples (Monograph, Lovable, Ramp) with Hugo.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=826.0)
  - [**Tyler:** Re-engage with Hugo early next year upon the launch of GrowthX's new AI visibility product.](https://fathom.video/share/T13PCheg9_3qz_JnGfDg385xM_D4cCGr?tab=summary&timestamp=816.0)

action_items.description: Email Hugo GrowthX examples: business travel guides, Lovable, Augment Code, Ramp
